title: Navigation Feedback
description: Flash a landed target after TOC, footnote, search, or custom navigation jumps.

files.content:
---
title: Navigation Feedback
description: Flash a landed target after TOC, footnote, search, or custom navigation jumps.
---

<PackageInfo>

## Features

- Briefly highlight the landed node after a navigation jump.
- Replace any previous flash deterministically instead of stacking timers.
- Expose editor methods for flash-only and selection-driven navigation flows.
- Inject `data-nav-*` attributes so custom renders can style the active target.

</PackageInfo>

Navigation feedback is a small core plugin for “you landed here” UX.

Use it when your feature jumps to a document target and you want short visual
confirmation without changing the meaning of the selection itself.

That makes it a good fit for:

- table of contents jumps
- footnote navigation
- search and find-jump flows
- custom anchor or outline surfaces

## Manual Usage

<Steps>

### Core Plugin

`NavigationFeedbackPlugin` is part of Plate core and is included automatically
when you create your editor with `createPlateEditor`.

You do not need to add it to the `plugins` array just to use the default
behavior.

### Configure Duration

If you want to change the flash duration, configure the core plugin through the
root plugin:

```tsx
import { createPlateEditor, NavigationFeedbackPlugin } from 'platejs/react';

const editor = createPlateEditor({
  rootPlugin: (plugin) =>
    plugin.configurePlugin(NavigationFeedbackPlugin, {
      options: {
        duration: 1200,
      },
    }),
});
```

- `options.duration`: Default flash duration in milliseconds.
  - **Default:** `800`

### Flash a Target

Use `editor.tf.navigation.flashTarget(...)` when the jump is already handled and
you only want visual confirmation on the landed node.

```tsx
editor.tf.navigation.flashTarget({
  target: {
    path: [12],
    type: 'node',
  },
});
```

You can also override the default duration or highlight variant per call:

```tsx
editor.tf.navigation.flashTarget({
  duration: 1500,
  target: {
    path: [12],
    type: 'node',
  },
  variant: 'navigated',
});
```

### Navigate and Flash in One Call

Use `editor.tf.navigation.navigate(...)` when the same action should:

- move the selection
- focus the editor
- scroll the target into view
- flash the landed node

```tsx
editor.tf.navigation.navigate({
  select: {
    anchor: { offset: 0, path: [12, 0] },
    focus: { offset: 0, path: [12, 0] },
  },
  target: {
    path: [12],
    type: 'node',
  },
});
```

You can tune the behavior:

```tsx
editor.tf.navigation.navigate({
  flash: {
    duration: 1200,
    variant: 'navigated',
  },
  focus: true,
  scroll: true,
  scrollTarget: { offset: 0, path: [12, 0] },
  select: { offset: 0, path: [12, 0] },
  target: {
    path: [12],
    type: 'node',
  },
});
```

- `flash`: Per-call flash config. Set `false` to skip the visual feedback.
- `focus`: Focus the editor after navigation.
  - **Default:** `true`
- `scroll`: Scroll the target into view.
  - **Default:** `true`
- `scrollTarget`: Use a specific point for scrolling instead of deriving one
  from the target or selection.
- `select`: Point or range to apply before the flash.

### Style the Landed Target

The plugin injects transient attributes on the active node:

- `data-nav-highlight`
- `data-nav-cycle`
- `data-nav-pulse`
- `data-nav-target`

It also injects the CSS variable:

- `--plate-nav-feedback-duration`

The Plate docs site styles these attributes in its own `globals.css`, but you can style them inside any editor shell:

```css
.slate-editor [data-nav-highlight] {
  border-radius: 0.375rem;
}

.slate-editor [data-nav-highlight][data-nav-cycle='0'] {
  animation: plate-nav-highlight-a var(--plate-nav-feedback-duration, 900ms)
    ease-out;
}

.slate-editor [data-nav-highlight][data-nav-cycle='1'] {
  animation: plate-nav-highlight-b var(--plate-nav-feedback-duration, 900ms)
    ease-out;
}
```

</Steps>

## Plugins

### `NavigationFeedbackPlugin`

Core plugin for transient “landed target” feedback after successful navigation.

<API name="NavigationFeedbackPlugin">
<APIOptions>
  <APIItem name="duration" type="number" optional>
    Default flash duration in milliseconds.
    - **Default:** `800`
  </APIItem>
</APIOptions>
</API>

## API

### `api.navigation.activeTarget`

Get the current flashed target, if any.

<API name="activeTarget">
<APIReturns>
  <APIItem name="return" type="NavigationFeedbackActiveTarget | null">
    Current navigation target metadata, or `null` when no flash is active.
  </APIItem>
</APIReturns>
</API>

### `api.navigation.clear`

Clear the current navigation feedback target immediately.

<API name="clear" />

### `api.navigation.isTarget`

Check whether a path is the current flashed target.

<API name="isTarget">
<APIParameters>
  <APIItem name="path" type="Path">
    Path to compare against the current active target.
  </APIItem>
</APIParameters>
</API>

## Transforms

### `tf.navigation.clear`

Clear the current flashed target immediately.

<API name="clear" />

### `tf.navigation.flashTarget`

Flash a target node without changing selection, focus, or scroll behavior.

<API name="flashTarget">
<APIParameters>
  <APIItem name="target" type="{ path: Path; type: 'node' }">
    Node target to flash.
  </APIItem>
  <APIItem name="duration" type="number" optional>
    Override the default flash duration for this call.
  </APIItem>
  <APIItem name="variant" type="string" optional>
    Highlight variant stored in `data-nav-highlight`.
    - **Default:** `navigated`
  </APIItem>
</APIParameters>
</API>

### `tf.navigation.navigate`

Select, focus, scroll, and flash a navigation target in one call.

<API name="navigate">
<APIParameters>
  <APIItem name="target" type="{ path: Path; type: 'node' }">
    Node target to navigate to.
  </APIItem>
  <APIItem name="select" type="Point | Range" optional>
    Point or range to apply before scrolling and flashing.
  </APIItem>
  <APIItem name="focus" type="boolean" optional>
    Focus the editor after selection.
    - **Default:** `true`
  </APIItem>
  <APIItem name="scroll" type="boolean" optional>
    Scroll the target into view.
    - **Default:** `true`
  </APIItem>
  <APIItem name="scrollTarget" type="Point" optional>
    Explicit point to scroll into view.
  </APIItem>
  <APIItem name="flash" type="false | { duration?: number; variant?: string }" optional>
    Flash configuration for this navigation call.
  </APIItem>
</APIParameters>
</API>
